findings.title: Instructure / Canvas LMS Data Breach — ShinyHunters Claims ~275M Records Across 9,000 Schools, Vulnerability-Driven
findings.affected_technology: Instructure Canvas Learning Management System (dominant North-American higher-ed LMS plus widespread K-12 and corporate-training tenants); third-party Canvas integrators (LTI / OAuth bridges)
findings.summary: Instructure confirmed a vulnerability-driven breach and ShinyHunters is now extorting the company on its leak site, claiming ~275M records across ~9,000 schools / 15,000 institutions including names, emails, student IDs, and private user-to-user messages.

findings.title: Apache OpenNLP Triple Advisory — Arbitrary Class Instantiation, XXE, and OOM via Malicious Model Files (CVE-2026-42027 / CVE-2026-40682 / CVE-2026-42440)
findings.affected_technology: Apache OpenNLP (Java NLP toolkit) <2.5.9 and 3.0 <3.0.0-M3 — embedded across data pipelines, Solr/Elasticsearch enrichment, Spark NLP wrappers, search-relevance harnesses, ML-as-a-Service products
findings.summary: Apache OpenNLP shipped 2.5.9 / 3.0.0-M3 fixing three independent bugs all reachable via a malicious model archive: arbitrary-class instantiation in ExtensionLoader (static initializers run pre-allowlist), XXE in DictionaryEntryPersistor, and OOM-DoS in AbstractModelReader unsigned-int allocations.

findings.title: Apache Polaris Metadata Path Validation Bypass — CVE-2026-42809 / 42810 / 42811 / 42812 (Iceberg Catalog)
findings.affected_technology: Apache Polaris <1.4.1 (open-source REST catalog brokering IAM-authenticated Iceberg-table access on S3/GCS/Azure cloud-object storage; multi-tenant data-lake trust boundary)
findings.summary: ALTER TABLE updates touching only write.metadata.path bypass Polaris commit-time location validation under allow.unstructured.table.location=true, letting a table-modify attacker redirect metadata writes plus issued cloud credentials to attacker-chosen storage.

findings.title: Microsoft Defender Wrongly Flags DigiCert Root Certificates as Trojan:Win32/Cerdigent.A!dha — Mass Cert Removal on Windows Fleets
findings.affected_technology: Microsoft Defender (consumer + Defender for Endpoint) on Windows; any Windows-side service validating against affected DigiCert root/intermediate CA certificates (browsers, .NET apps, OS update channels, signed-binary verification, smart-card flows)
findings.summary: Defender's signature update misclassifies legitimate DigiCert root/intermediate CAs as a trojan and removes them from the Windows certificate store — pure operational disruption (failed updates, broken TLS chains) at fleet scale; pause autoroll on a control group.

noted.product: Apache Atlas (0.8 → 2.4.0) DSL search endpoint script injection
noted.summary: Script injection in Apache Atlas DSL search endpoint disclosed via oss-security 2026-05-03; technical detail thin on public list — Atlas is the Hadoop-ecosystem metadata governance system and DSL-evaluated query injection is a familiar pattern, watch for the upstream advisory and patch.

noted.product: Starlet Perl PSGI server HTTP request smuggling (≤0.31)
noted.summary: Content-Length is incorrectly prioritised over Transfer-Encoding: chunked, violating RFC 7230 §3.3.3 — niche Perl PSGI server but classic request-smuggling primitive; anyone running a reverse proxy in front of Starlet is exposed, patch shipped, upgrade.

noted.product: Linux kernel io_uring zcrx freelist OOB write
noted.summary: Reported on oss-security 2026-05-03; LPE-class — adding to watchlist alongside the recently-KEV'd CopyFail (CVE-2026-31431) since io_uring continues to be a fertile LPE source, track for KEV addition.

noted.product: Vim < 9.2.0435 — OS command injection via 'path' completion
noted.summary: Local-only desktop-tool issue; out of remote-services scope but worth flagging because Vim is everywhere on Linux ops boxes and any chain that lands shell on a developer/ops workstation can pivot via vim.

noted.product: Vietnamese-linked Facebook account compromise via Google AppSheet (~30,000 accounts)
noted.summary: Vietnamese threat actors used Google AppSheet as a phishing-relay platform to harvest ~30,000 Facebook accounts — adds Google's no-code automation surface to the SaaS-as-phishing-relay pattern (Pipedream/Zapier/Power Automate); tune phishing-URL rules to include *.appsheet.com redirects.

noted.product: Microsoft Entra ID — AI-agent role privilege-escalation patched
noted.summary: Microsoft patched a flaw in the Entra ID AI-agent role enabling privilege escalation / role takeover (per SCWorld 2026-05-02); CVE not surfaced in observed snippet, MSRC source returned no detail via WebFetch — track for formal MSRC advisory.

noted.product: SonicWall firmware roll-up referenced on SC Media 2026-05-02
noted.summary: Three CVEs patched; appears to be follow-on coverage of the SonicOS April 2026 roll-up already covered 2026-05-01 (CVE-2026-0204 et al.) — no material new info, flagged in case yesterday's report was missed.

Federal remediation deadline already past (KEV addition 2026-04-29); re-flagging because SC Media headlined it again 2026-05-02 — if any unpatched ScreenConnect <23.9.8 remains in your fleet, treat as compromised.

noted.product: WordPress plugin batch — Gravity Forms 7.2 / Widget Options 8.8 / WCFM Frontend Manager 8.1 / Nex-Forms Ultimate 7.2 (dbugs.ptsecurity)
noted.summary: Latest Positive Technologies dbugs advisories in past 48h; Gravity Forms in particular is a high-deployment commercial plugin used widely on enterprise WordPress sites — check the dbugs feed for fix versions and patch.
